title: Clearing doctor/date does not invalidate an in-flight slot request
reasoning: The stale-response guard only increments latestRequestId after both doctorId and date are present. If a user selects a doctor/date, a request starts, and then clears either field before the request resolves, the early return path does not increment latestRequestId. The old response still matches latestRequestId.current and can repopulate slots for a doctor/date that is no longer selected. This creates stale UI state and can carry old slot data into the next interaction path.
reproduction: Render SlotPicker with doctorId/date, delay api.getAvailableSlots, then rerender with doctorId or date missing before the promise resolves. Resolve the original promise with slots. The component calls setSlots with the stale response because latestRequestId was not invalidated on the missing-input branch.
recommendation: Increment or otherwise invalidate latestRequestId before returning when doctorId or date is missing, and consider clearing loading there as well. A cleanup flag or AbortController around the request would also prevent late writes after the active selection changes.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for SlotPicker or its async request ordering behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a SlotPicker test that starts a slot fetch, rerenders with a missing doctorId/date before resolution, resolves the original request, and asserts stale slots are not stored/rendered when valid inputs are later restored.
minimumFixScope: Update SlotPicker's useEffect invalidation logic for the !doctorId || !date branch and cover it with one async interaction test.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists at src/pages/components/SlotPicker.jsx. The missing-input branch at lines 10-15 has changed from only clearing slots and returning to incrementing latestRequestId.current, clearing slots, setting loading false, and returning. The in-flight request path still captures const requestId = ++latestRequestId.current at line 18 and checks requestId against latestRequestId.current before writing slots or fallback data at lines 25 and 33, with loading cleared only for the current request at lines 36-37. Therefore, clearing doctorId or date now invalidates any older in-flight request before it can repopulate stale slots. I found no test directories or test script in package.json; npm run lint could not be executed because the command was blocked by policy, so this conclusion is based on current code evidence rather than a passing regression test.
commands: rg -n "SlotPicker|available slots|Select a doctor and date first|getAvailableSlots" -S src test tests __tests__
